commit.message:
Add cross-query consistency tests to catch contradictory agent responses

Tests verify that different query paths (direct priors query vs all-data
query, whereabouts listing vs city search, etc.) return consistent answers
for the same target. Catches bugs like the system denying a target has
priors then revealing them when asked for all historical data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: merge technology platform sections + fix card alignment across page

Combine SystemArchitecture and FutureEcosystem into a unified PlatformEcosystem
component with B2B-focused hardware layer, connectivity layer, intelligent
software suite (Dashboard + iOS App), and data value proposition. Fix card
alignment issues in SensingScience (BCG/ECG, Respiratory/PSG, Ultra-High
Sensitivity) and ClinicalValidation (CSA/OSA, HRV Poincaré) using flex-col
+ flex-1 pattern for consistent image-text alignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: first landing page

commit.message:
Initial commit: Wallet Tracer - sybil farming detection for ERC-20 contracts

Backend: FastAPI + aiohttp for raw JSON-RPC calls to Geth node
- Transfer event batching with automatic chunk halving on overflow
- In-memory clustering by shared destination wallet
- Async batched balance fetching with semaphore concurrency control

Frontend: React + Vite + Tailwind CSS with dark theme
- Input form with 3 date modes (single day, range, last 7 days)
- Dashboard with summary cards, sortable cluster table, and pagination
- Cluster drill-down with wallet details and Etherscan links
- CSV export functionality

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump all versions to 0.1.17, update changelog

The companion integration manifest must be bumped so the install
script copies updated files to HA's custom_components directory.

commit.message:
Food Prep Watcher: detect overwritten files by modifiedTime + set default folder

- Switch from file ID deduplication to modifiedTime comparison so the
  same overwritten PDF is picked up every 2 minutes when its content changes
- Set default folder ID to 1lfiinyI5VaZY9eWgpEaSUVxrQljOOTfp and auto-save
  it to Firestore config on first run so the UI shows it
- History entries now keyed by fileId+modifiedTime to preserve a record
  of each version

harden .gitignore for growing repo — expand secret, cache, and artifact coverage (#17)

Add ignore rules for: crypto material (.p12, .pfx, .jks), auth tokens,
serialized objects (.pickle), linter/type-checker caches (.ruff_cache,
.mypy_cache, .pyright), Jupyter checkpoints, IaC state files (Terraform,
Pulumi), Docker overrides, merge artifacts (.bak, .orig), and archives.

commit.message:
feat: frontend-to-backend price feed for geo-blocked Binance

Railway US can't reach Binance API (geo-restriction). Solution:
- Frontend browser connects to Binance WS (no geo-block for users)
- Frontend sends price to backend every 5s via POST /market/price-feed
- Backend caches price in Redis (TTL 30s)
- get_ticker_price reads from Redis when Binance API is unreachable
- Paper trading + close position + engine all use Redis-cached price

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix teacher avatar not loading in Filament by setting disk to public

The FileUpload component defaulted to the 'local' disk but avatars are
stored on the 'public' disk. Also fixed directory name to use hyphens
(quran-teachers) matching the web frontend upload path.

Users now see abstract "credits" on the billing page instead of raw API
costs. Costs are stored internally in dollars and converted at the display
layer using a configurable multiplier (1 unit = 1,000 credits).

commit.message:
Track E: parsing completeness for parseLiteralsSection (raw/RLE) (#1159)

* refactor: compress proof bodies in Zstd.lean with tactic macro and helper lemma

Extract two repeated patterns found across ~20 frame-level theorems:

1. `frame_from_blocks` tactic macro: replaces the identical 19-line
   frame-unwinding ending (unfold decompressFrame, substitute header,
   handle dictionary check, close with grind) with a single macro call.

2. `parseBlockHeader_data_not_le` lemma: combines parseBlockHeader_le_size
   and parseBlockHeader_pos_eq into a single reusable lemma, replacing
   the 5-line `hoff` derivation block.

Net reduction: 3957 → 3551 lines (−406 lines, −10.3%).
Sorry count unchanged (4, all in XxHash).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for Zstd.lean review session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* review: clean up stale issue queue (#1146)

* refactor: ZstdSequence proof quality improvements

- Extract `resolveSequenceFseTables_destruct` to deduplicate the 3-call
  destructuring boilerplate shared by `resolveSequenceFseTables_pos_ge`
  and `resolveSequenceFseTables_valid`
- Simplify `hbeq` pattern in `parseSequencesHeader_numSeq_small` and
  `parseSequencesHeader_numSeq_medium` from verbose `intro heq; exact
  absurd (eq_of_beq heq) (by omega)` to `simp only [beq_iff_eq]; omega`
- Merge consecutive `rw` calls in both parseSequencesHeader proofs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for ZstdSequence proof quality review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: resolveSingleFseTable FSE-compressed mode + decodeSequencesWF zero-case completeness (#1134)

* feat: parseLiteralsSection completeness for compressed literals (litType=2)

Add `parseLiteralsSection_succeeds_compressed` theorem proving that when
litType=2 (compressed with new Huffman tree), the header parses, tree
descriptor succeeds, and Huffman decoding succeeds, then
`parseLiteralsSection` returns the exact expected output. The
`prevHuffTree` parameter is universally quantified since litType=2 never
uses it.

Completes the compressed literals path, complementing the existing
treeless (litType=3) theorem and the in-progress raw/RLE theorems.

Closes #1119

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for session 6626fe38

* Track E: resolveSequenceFseTables composed completeness (#1123)

* feat: resolveSequenceFseTables composed completeness theorem

When each of the three resolveSingleFseTable calls succeeds (for literal
lengths, offsets, and match lengths), the composed resolveSequenceFseTables
succeeds and returns the exact output. Connects individual mode completeness
to the composed function via simple monadic unfolding.

Closes #1120

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for resolveSequenceFseTables completeness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Meditate: 20-PR batch — parsing completeness maturity, conflict patterns, content matrix next steps (#1127)

* feat: add lean-parsing-completeness skill

New skill documenting the proof pattern for parsing completeness theorems —
showing that well-formed input implies native parser success. Covers the
core technique (unfold, guard discharge, case-split intermediates, extract
equalities), common sub-patterns (PUnit artifacts, getElem! bounds, BEq
bridging, sizeFormat dispatch), eliminator lemma factoring, and anti-patterns.

Distilled from 8+ parsing completeness theorems across DEFLATE and Zstd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add congrArg and letFun patterns to lean-dependent-types skill

Two patterns that recurred across multiple sessions but weren't documented:

1. congrArg for large list/array rewrites: when rw hits maxRecDepth on
   288-element lists, use congrArg to lift equality through a function
   wrapper instead.

2. letFun for have bindings: when unfold leaves have bindings in the
   elaborated term, use simp only [letFun] to reduce them. The linter
   false positive on this is noted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add join point and List.replicate patterns to lean-monad-proofs skill

Three additions based on recurring struggles across multiple sessions:

1. Join points (__do_jp): documents how early return in do-blocks creates
   join points that cause unfold mismatches, with the rewrite to avoid them.

2. List.replicate expansion: documents how unfold expands replicate into
   large literal lists, with the definitional-alias workaround.

3. Cross-reference to new lean-parsing-completeness skill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for meditate session #1125

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: BackwardBitReader.init completeness theorems (#1126)

Prove that `highBitPos` returns `some` for nonzero bytes, and that
`BackwardBitReader.init` succeeds when the input range is valid and
the last byte is nonzero. These are needed to show backward bitstream
initialization succeeds for well-formed Zstd compressed blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for BackwardBitReader.init completeness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1130)

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1128)

Add two completeness theorems for the FSE-coded Huffman weight path:

- `parseHuffmanWeightsFse_succeeds`: when data has enough bytes and all
  sub-calls (decodeFseDistribution, buildFseTable, BackwardBitReader.init,
  decodeFseSymbolsAll) succeed, parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds
- `parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_succeeds_fse`: when headerByte ∈ 1..127,
  parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds, and buildZstdHuffmanTable succeeds,
  parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor succeeds

This completes parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor coverage for all paths:
- Direct (headerByte ≥ 128): merged in #1118
- FSE (headerByte ∈ 1..127): this commit
- Error (headerByte = 0): always fails, no theorem needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path completeness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: resolveSingleFseTable FSE-compressed completeness + decodeSequencesWF zero-case (#1132)

Add completeness theorems for the remaining simple cases in sequence
decoding pipeline:

- resolveSingleFseTable_succeeds_fse: FSE-compressed mode succeeds when
  decodeFseDistribution and buildFseTable both succeed
- decodeSequencesWF_succeeds_zero: zero-sequence case returns empty array

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Track E: decodeHuffmanLiterals completeness (#1135)

* feat: decodeHuffmanLiterals completeness for single and four-stream paths

Add two completeness theorems for the Huffman literal decoding dispatch function:
- `decodeHuffmanLiterals_succeeds_single`: single-stream path succeeds when
  BackwardBitReader.init and decodeHuffmanStream succeed
- `decodeHuffmanLiterals_succeeds_four`: four-stream path succeeds when
  decodeFourHuffmanStreams succeeds

Note: `decodeHuffmanStreamWF_size` (deliverable 3 in #1129) already existed.

Closes #1099

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add congrArg and letFun patterns to lean-dependent-types skill

Two patterns that recurred across multiple sessions but weren't documented:

1. congrArg for large list/array rewrites: when rw hits maxRecDepth on
   288-element lists, use congrArg to lift equality through a function
   wrapper instead.

2. letFun for have bindings: when unfold leaves have bindings in the
   elaborated term, use simp only [letFun] to reduce them. The linter
   false positive on this is noted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add join point and List.replicate patterns to lean-monad-proofs skill

Three additions based on recurring struggles across multiple sessions:

1. Join points (__do_jp): documents how early return in do-blocks creates
   join points that cause unfold mismatches, with the rewrite to avoid them.

2. List.replicate expansion: documents how unfold expands replicate into
   large literal lists, with the definitional-alias workaround.

3. Cross-reference to new lean-parsing-completeness skill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for meditate session #1125

equalities), common sub-patterns (PUnit artifacts, getElem! bounds, BEq
bridging, sizeFormat dispatch), eliminator lemma factoring, and anti-patterns.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1130)

* feat: parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor FSE-path + parseHuffmanWeightsFse completeness (#1128)

Add two completeness theorems for the FSE-coded Huffman weight path:

- `parseHuffmanWeightsFse_succeeds`: when data has enough bytes and all
  sub-calls (decodeFseDistribution, buildFseTable, BackwardBitReader.init,
  decodeFseSymbolsAll) succeed, parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds
- `parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor_succeeds_fse`: when headerByte ∈ 1..127,
  parseHuffmanWeightsFse succeeds, and buildZstdHuffmanTable succeeds,
  parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor succeeds

This completes parseHuffmanTreeDescriptor coverage for all paths:
- Direct (headerByte ≥ 128): merged in #1118
- FSE (headerByte ∈ 1..127): this commit
- Error (headerByte = 0): always fails, no theorem needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add direct system tools: shell, files, search, Mac control, AppleScript

Vance can now directly operate the Mac without routing everything through
Claude Code. New tools: run_shell (any terminal command), read_file,
write_file, list_directory, search_files (grep+find), system_info
(CPU/RAM/disk/battery/network), open_app (apps/URLs/files), and
run_applescript (notifications, clipboard, window mgmt, Finder).
Claude Code reserved for complex multi-step AI coding tasks only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Prevent product cards from overlapping on same grid position

Fixed bug where new products were assigned positions based on array index
without checking if that position was already occupied by another product.

Now properly finds the first unoccupied position by:
1. Getting all currently occupied positions from existing state
2. Finding the first available position starting from 0
3. Marking each position as occupied when assigned

Ensures only one card can occupy a grid index at a time.

Fix iOS keyboard properly: sticky input inside scrollable container

Instead of JS hacks, put compose-input-area inside compose-scroll-body
with position:sticky;bottom:0. iOS naturally scrolls the container to
show the focused input — no visualViewport manipulation needed.

commit.message:
feat(manman): UI restyling, log histogram viewer, named volume installation, volume_id routing (#382)

* fix(s3): always use primary endpoint for presigned PUT URLs

OVH's public endpoint (cloud.ovh.us) does not reliably handle S3
presigned PUT operations. The host-manager is internal infrastructure
that can reach the primary S3 endpoint (io.cloud.ovh.us) directly,
so always use the primary presign client rather than presignPublic.

commit.message:
fix(presidio): remove noisy entities, fix test inputs, raise timeout (#13)

- Remove PERSON and DATE_TIME from PRESIDIO_ENTITIES: spaCy en_core_web_lg
  produces excessive false positives for both, rejecting clean text
- Fix SSN test input: 123-45-6789 is sequential and Presidio rejects it as
  an invalid SSN pattern; use 534-09-8231 instead
- Fix phone test input: (555) numbers fictional-use, score below 0.7 threshold;
  use a real area code (914) instead
- Raise testTimeout/hookTimeout to 60s: Presidio Lambda cold start adds
  8-15s on top of existing Bedrock latency
- Replace brittle stats count assertion with capture success check to avoid
  S3 Vectors eventual-consistency races in shared CI environment

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix critical balance bug, add EV-based adaptive optimization, improve trade display

- Fix double-deduction of entry notional in balance tracking (was causing
  all accounts to appear blown when strategy is actually profitable)
- Reduce slippage from 2% to 1% each way for more realistic modeling
- Rewrite AdaptiveFilters to use EV-based approach: only blocks conditions
  with negative expected value, scales sizing 0.5x-2x based on relative EV
- Add replay-based adaptive mode: each run replays same data from start
  with progressively better filters (vs old approach of continuing forward)
- Add backtest optimizer for per-dimension statistical analysis
- Improve dashboard trade table: shows date/time with year, symbol,
  CALL/PUT, strike, expiry, entry/exit prices, PnL (color coded),
  time held, and letter grade
- Add per-run distinct colors in win rate and equity curve charts
- Add strategy optimizer endpoint and dashboard UI

commit.message:
feat(EPIC-013): implement SPEC-029, SPEC-030, SPEC-032 for ADR-005 pipeline unification

SPEC-029 (Catalog Entry Schema Validation):
- Make quality_attributes always present in catalog-entry.template.j2
- Add catalog-schema.yaml validation schema to discover skill references

SPEC-030 (Pipeline Runtime Prompt Assembly):
- Refactor classify-tooluse.sh to assemble system prompt from discover
  skill files (SKILL.md + styles.md + catalog-entry.template.j2) at
  runtime instead of reading pipeline/prompts/system-prompt-tooluse.md
- Add --show-prompt flag for debugging assembled prompts

SPEC-032 (Legacy Pipeline Retirement):
- Remove 14 legacy pipeline files: system-prompt-tooluse.md,
  system-prompt.md, response-schema.json, validation-prompt.md,
  apply-review.py, apply-tooluse-result.py, llm-review.sh, classify.py,
  run-pipeline.sh, llm-validate.sh, validate-review.sh, validate-review.py,
  stamp-deep-validation.py, calibrate-confidence.sh

commit.message:
Initial release: NapNav — Smart Flight Planning for Families

- 5-step form: children sleep profiles, trip details, logistics, flight duration
- Auto timezone detection from 130+ airport database (Haversine distance)
- Auto flight duration estimation (great circle distance + cruising speed)
- Flight window generator: scores every 15-min slot, returns top 5 ranked 2-hour windows
- Interactive heatmap: explore all departure times with click-to-detail panel
- Score delta badges: every +/- point explained inline
- Body clock mismatch detection for multi-timezone travel
- Pre-trip checklist and expert tips (personalized per child needs)
- Responsive, print-friendly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: load root .env in drizzle.config.ts for monorepo compatibility

drizzle-kit runs from packages/db and doesn't automatically resolve
the root .env. Use dotenv to explicitly load ../../.env so DATABASE_URL
is available. Also add dotenv and tsx as proper package.json deps.

commit.message:
Stop enforcing Facebook nodes in normalizeGDriveLayout

Facebook nodes are user-configurable — they should not be forced back
onto the canvas when deleted. Only core pipeline nodes (Google Drive,
Save PDFs) are now enforced. Edges to/from Facebook nodes are also
excluded from enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enrich vision, alerts & RAG with detailed context fields

Vision prompt now requests 3-5 sentence descriptions with time_of_day,
people_count, equipment_types, vegetation_damage, damage_area_estimate.
Alert prompt uses boolean flags for concrete threat descriptions.
RAG gets machinery/equipment context + auto-hint for qualified offence
(ч. 3 ст. 260 УК РФ). All callers and frontend updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bake generation version into URL builders for automatic cache busting

pokeproxyImageUrl and pokeproxySvgUrl now accept an optional version
param, so any component building these URLs gets cache-busted without
manual query string concatenation. getCardImageUrl also uses the
global generation version for grid/tile image URLs.

Fix Excel parsing: Milestones and SS tabs now populate correctly

- parseMilestonesSheet: rewritten with raw:true index-based access for
  performance on 107K rows; UTC-safe serial→week conversion avoids
  timezone drift
- parseSSSheet: raw:true with direct numeric MRR and serial date handling
- Fix sheet name collision: use exact match 'milestones' instead of
  includes('milestone') so BC GTM Milestones and Milestones by Month
  no longer overwrite parsed data with empty results
- Fix toMonthISO: handle 2-digit years (MM/DD/YY format)
- Remove unused col() helper
